title: ferray-random: multivariate_normal takes flat cov slice instead of Array<f64, Ix2>
description:
Current state: 

Desired state: 

Reason: 

multivariate_normal() takes mean: &[f64] and cov: &[f64] (flattened row-major) instead of Array types. NumPy accepts array_like for both. This is an ergonomic issue — the user must flatten their covariance matrix manually. It also means the function has its own internal Cholesky decomposition (cholesky_decompose in multivariate.rs) instead of reusing ferray-linalg::cholesky. Accept Array<f64, Ix2> for cov and Array<f64, Ix1> for mean, delegating to ferray-linalg for Cholesky.
